senses.gloss: to work hard, to toil, to exert oneself, to slog

examples.text: وَمَا كَانَ مِنَ ٱلْحَيَوَانِ صُلْبًا يَرْعَى‭‬ فِي ٱلْمَرَاعِي ٱلْيَابِسَةِ ٱلصُّلْبَةِ وَيَكُدُّ‬ فِي ٱلْأَعْمَالِ ٱلصَّعْبَةِ‭‬ فَإِنَّهَا أَصْلَبُ وَأَبْطَأُ ٱسْتِمْرَاءً
examples.english: What there is of sturdy animals grazing the dry and harsh pastures and exerting themselves in hard works, they are tough and tardy (when to be digested).

senses.gloss: to make weary, to tire, to goad

examples.text: لَا بُدَّ أَنَّهُ شَيْءٌ مَوْجُودٌ، مِنْ حَجَرٍ وَتُرَابٍ وَمَاءٍ وَسَمَاءٍ، وَلَيْسَتْ مِثْلَمَا تَهُومُ فِي رَأْسِهِ ٱلْمَكْدُودِ
examples.english: It was certainly something real, of stones, earth, water, and sky, not as it slumbered in his troubled mind.

etymology.text: Borrowed from Aramaic כּדּא (kaddā, “pot, pitcher”), ultimately from Old Tamil 𑀓𑀺𑀡𑁰𑀝𑀺 (kiṇṭi, “pitcher pot”). Doublet of قَادُوس (qādūs).

body.text: [inf. n. of 1, q. v. ―         -b2-  as a subst.]
A mortar in which things are pounded,
